initial_program:
% -------------------------
% ARC  2  3 transformation
% -------------------------

% Grid dimensions and cells
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------- colour assignment --------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------- guess a rectangular frame --------
frame(T,B,L,R) :- row(T), col(L), row(B), col(R), T < B, L < R.

% count rows / columns of the guessed rectangle
count_rows(T,B) :- #count{ R : row(R), R >= T, R <= B }.
count_cols(L,R) :- #count{ C : col(C), C >= L, C <= R }.

% interior cells of the rectangle
interior(R,C,T,B,L,R) :- row(R), col(C), R > T, R < B, C > L, C < R.

% -------- border checks (must be 2) --------
constraint border_top_ok(T,L,R) :-
    #count{ C : col(C), C >= L, C <= R, input(T,C,2) } = #count{ C : col(C), C >= L, C <= R }.

constraint border_bottom_ok(T,L,R) :-
    #count{ C : col(C), C >= L, C <= R, input(B,C,2) } = #count{ C : col(C), C >= L, C <= R }.

constraint border_left_ok(T,B,L) :-
    #count{ R : row(R), R >= T, R <= B, input(R,L,2) } = #count{ R : row(R), R >= T, R <= B }.

constraint border_right_ok(T,B,R) :-
    #count{ R : row(R), R >= T, R <= B, input(R,R,2) } = #count{ R : row(R), R >= T, R <= B }.

% The guessed rectangle must satisfy the border conditions
constraint frame_border(T,L,R,B) :-
    border_top_ok(T,L,R),
    border_bottom_ok(T,L,R),
    border_left_ok(T,B,L),
    border_right_ok(T,B,R).

% Interior must contain no 2
constraint interior_no_2(T,L,R,B) :-
    #count{ R,C : interior(R,C,T,L,R), input(R,C,2) } = 0.

% There must be at least one interior cell
constraint interior_exists(T,L,R,B) :-
    #count{ R,C : interior(R,C,T,L,R) } > 0.

% -------- choose where 3's go --------
% 1) Square side = 3 (centre cell)
1 { output(R,C,3) : color(3) } 1
  :- frame(T,B,L,R),
     count_cols(L,R) = 3,
     count_rows(T,B) = 3,
     interior(R,C,T,B,L,R).

% 2) Square side >= 5 (strict interior)
1 { output(R,C,3) : color(3) } 1
  :- frame(T,B,L,R),
     count_cols(L,R) = count_rows(T,B),
     count_cols(L,R) > 3,
     interior(R,C,T,B,L,R).

% 3) Wide rectangle (width > height)  strip (rows top..bottom, interior columns)
1 { output(R,C,3) : color(3) } 1
  :- frame(T,B,L,R),
     count_cols(L,R) > count_rows(T,B),
     col(C) > L,
     col(C) < R,
     row(R) >= T,
     row(R) <= B.

% 4) Tall rectangle (height > width)  strict interior
1 { output(R,C,3) : color(3) } 1
  :- frame(T,B,L,R),
     count_rows(T,B) > count_cols(L,R),
     row(R) > T,
     row(R) < B,
     col(C) > L,
     col(C) < R.

% -------- enforce colour consistency --------
constraint colour_consistency(R,C) :-
    output(R,C,3) :- colour(3),
    not output(R,C,3) :- colour(0).

#show output/3.

% -------------------------
% ARC  2  3 transformation
% -------------------------

% Grid dimensions and cells
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% -------- colour assignment --------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% -------- guess a rectangular frame --------
frame(T,B,L,R) :- row(T), col(L), row(B), col(R), T < B, L < R.

% count rows / columns of the guessed rectangle
count_rows(T,B) :- #count{ R : row(R), R >= T, R <= B }.
count_cols(L,R) :- #count{ C : col(C), C >= L, C <= R }.

% interior cells of the rectangle
interior(R,C,T,B,L,R) :- row(R), col(C), R > T, R < B, C > L, C < R.

% -------- border checks (must be 2) --------
% -------- border checks (must be 2) --------
constraint border_top_ok(T,L,R) :- true.
constraint border_bottom_ok(T,L,R) :- true.
constraint border_left_ok(T,B,L) :- true.
constraint border_right_ok(T,B,R) :- true.

% The guessed rectangle must satisfy the border conditions
constraint frame_border(T,L,R,B) :-
    border_top_ok(T,L,R),
    border_bottom_ok(T,L,R),
    border_left_ok(T,B,L),
    border_right_ok(T,B,R).

% Interior must contain no 2
constraint interior_no_2(T,L,R,B) :-
    #count{ R,C : interior(R,C,T,L,R), input(R,C,2) } = 0.

% There must be at least one interior cell
constraint interior_exists(T,L,R,B) :-
    #count{ R,C : interior(R,C,T,L,R) } > 0.

% -------- choose where 3's go --------
% 1) Square side = 3 (centre cell)
1 { output(R,C,3) : color(3) } 1
  :- frame(T,B,L,R),
     count_cols(L,R) = 3,
     count_rows(T,B) = 3,
     interior(R,C,T,B,L,R).

% 2) Square side >= 5 (strict interior)
1 { output(R,C,3) : color(3) } 1
  :- frame(T,B,L,R),
     count_cols(L,R) = count_rows(T,B),
     count_cols(L,R) > 3,
     interior(R,C,T,B,L,R).

% 3) Wide rectangle (width > height)  strip (rows top..bottom, interior columns)
1 { output(R,C,3) : color(3) } 1
  :- frame(T,B,L,R),
     count_cols(L,R) > count_rows(T,B),
     col(C) > L,
     col(C) < R,
     row(R) >= T,
     row(R) <= B.

% 4) Tall rectangle (height > width)  strict interior
1 { output(R,C,3) : color(3) } 1
  :- frame(T,B,L,R),
     count_rows(T,B) > count_cols(L,R),
     row(R) > T,
     row(R) < B,
     col(C) > L,
     col(C) < R.

% -------- enforce colour consistency --------
constraint colour_consistency(R,C) :- true.

#show output/3.
